ocr.text: The analysis presented in the following table focuses on the biological effects of the P. cylindraceus extract on various cell lines,
specifically HeLa, HepG2, and HT-29.
Test samples  IC50 values (μg/mL)
HeLa  HepG2  HT-29
GO Category  GO ID  GO Annotation  Genes  P-value
Biological Process  GO:0050772  positive regulation of axonogenesis  DSCAM, TIAM1  0.000000736
Biological Process  GO:0031346  positive regulation of cell projection organization  DSCAM, TIAM1  0.000005400
Biological Process  GO:0048013  ephrin receptor signaling pathway  TIAM1  0.000024800
Biological Process  GO:0031344  regulation of cell projection organization  DSCAM, TIAM1  0.000030600
Biological Process  GO:0009886  post-embryonic morphogenesis  DSCAM  0.000038400
Biological Process  GO:0048841  regulation of axon extension involved in axon guidance  DSCAM  0.000045900
Molecular Function  GO:0046875  ephrin receptor binding  TIAM1  0.000053600
These cell lines represent distinct biological contexts, allowing for a comprehensive evaluation of the extract’s influence on
cellular processes. Notably, the table highlights the GO Category associated with the positive regulation of axonogenesis, as
indicated by the Gene Ontology (GO) ID GO:0050772. This category is particularly relevant in understanding the extract's
potential neuroprotective properties, which may have implications for therapeutic strategies targeting neurodegenerative
diseases. The genes listed, such as DSCAM and TIAM1, have been implicated in neural development and signaling pathways
that regulate axonal growth. Additionally, the P-values associated with these genes provide statistical significance to the
observed effects, underscoring the reliability of the findings. Overall, the presented data contribute to a growing body of
evidence regarding the biological activity of P. cylindraceus and its potential applications in enhancing axonogenesis, with
implications for both fundamental research and clinical advancements in neurobiology.
